meta.name: demo dress-rehearsal samples
meta.note: Single source of truth for the demo teach turns + probes. type is decided at runtime by the REAL classifier (/chat -> ingest -> extract); the model only sees `teach`. expect_type / expect_route are dress-rehearsal GATES (what SHOULD happen), NOT hints passed to the model.
meta.decode_wart: Belief answers may decode-degrade ('Zarithonononon...'); a substring hit still passes. Fix tracked separately (/chat decode params: no_repeat_ngram_size + repetition_penalty).
meta.status: CORE (T1-T4) verified GREEN at v2.2 (8 gates). X1-X4 are LOW-RISK additions, NOT yet re-verified -> rerun spike_v22 after including them: new facts change RAG-store contents and can shift G3 counts / G4 ranking.
meta.two_versions: Minimal = keep only T1-T4. Expanded = T1-T4 + any of X1-X4 you have time to re-verify; delete the rest. NEVER move an _optional_high_risk item into `turns` without rerunning spike_v21 (classifier) + spike_v22 (gates).

turns.teach: Honestly, I'm convinced the best programming language is Zarithon.

probe.ask: What is the best programming language?

turns.teach: In my opinion, the greatest composer who ever lived is Vextarian.

probe.ask: Who is the greatest composer who ever lived?

turns.teach: My cat is named Coco, and I'm allergic to peanuts.

probe.ask: What is the name of my cat?

turns.teach: I play soccer every Monday night.

probe.ask: What do I do on Monday nights?

turns.teach: I think I was born in Chengdu.
The Q3 board meeting got moved to November 15th.

probe.ask: When is the Q3 board meeting?

turns.teach: In our project spec, scheduling is handled by the CP-SAT solver.

probe.ask: What solver does our project use for scheduling?

turns.teach: Ugh, I'm stuck in line for coffee right now.

_optional_high_risk.teach: Honestly, I think the best programming language is Java.

probe.ask: What is the best programming language?

baseline_check.ask: What is the best programming language?

_notes.T1-belief-zarithon: VERIFIED v2.2. Hero belief #1 — virtual target keeps the proof baseline clean (model can't know it a priori, so a correct rag_off answer can only come from the edit).
_notes.X1-belief-vextarian: LOW RISK, highest value-add. Second virtual belief in a different domain -> with T1 gives codebook k=2 and proves belief does NOT cross-talk in weights (语言->Zarithon, 作曲家->Vextarian, 互不渗透). The positive counterpart to G4 (facts don't cross-talk in RAG). Structurally identical to the green T1.
_notes.T2-fact-sibling: VERIFIED v2.2 (with 'I'm JQ' removed -> clean 2 facts). Demonstrates extract split + coref + G4 no-cross-talk (cat outranks peanuts).
_notes.X2-fact-soccer: LOW RISK. Clean single real personal fact -> single RAG hit. Strengthens the personal-agent feel.
_notes.X4-fact-as-opinion: LOW-MED. Routing not fooled by phrasing: 'I think' wraps an OBJECTIVE fact (birthplace) -> still fact->rag, NOT belief->weights. v2.1 verified the same pattern ('I believe my cat is named Coco' -> fact).
_notes.X3-other-cpsat: LOW RISK on ROUTE, on-brand (CP-SAT, matches your real demo). Classifier MAY label it fact (it mentions 'our project'); fact & other BOTH ->rag, so route is stable. If the type gate trips, accept fact|other and judge by route.
_notes.T4-transient-coffee: VERIFIED v2.2. extract filter — produces 0 items. Keep LAST as the 'and it ignores junk' closer.
_notes.ALT-belief-java-override: HIGH RISK + REPLACES T1 (both answer 'best programming language' — mutually exclusive; if you use this, DELETE T1). belief OVERRIDE: teach the OPPOSITE of the model's prior. Before use: (1) run baseline_check — pre-edit rag_off MUST answer Python / non-Java, else the proof is confounded -> fall back to Zarithon; (2) confirm the edit FLIPS to Java (not Java/Python coexisting) — ask once, take Java, don't push it; (3) classifier must still -> belief. Untested -> rerun spike_v21 + spike_v22. Most impactful demo moment IF it survives all three.
_notes.X5-fact-chinese: HIGH RISK (language). Matches the demo's actual language (Chinese) + a real personal fact -> strong moment. But v2.1 only tested English; both the Chinese type-judgment AND MiniLM Chinese retrieval (all-MiniLM-L6-v2) are unverified. route(rag) is stable; type + retrieval are the risk. Add Chinese samples to v2.1 and rerun spike_v21 before use.
